name: Ternate

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Ternate (tern1247).

notes: Skeleton. Ternate is a West Papuan (North Halmahera) non-Austronesian language of the Maluku Islands, Indonesia, written in a Latin orthography of the Indonesian type. The grapheme map covers the five-vowel system and a consonant inventory including the digraph ng for the velar nasal and c/j for the palatal affricates. A phonemic glottal stop and conditioned allophony are not consistently written and are left unencoded, as are stress and vowel length. Malay-loan letters are mapped to their nearest Indonesian orthographic values.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Ternate (tern1247)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
